compile_error: TileLang LayoutInference failed at src/op/parallel.cc:690 with: Vector size 8 is not divisible by coalesced width 16.

resource_notes.change: Only the six all64 global-weight-to-shared T.copy operations were changed: gate0, up0, gate1, up1, down0, and down1 explicitly pass coalesced_width=16. Input and up_logits copies to fragments, tiles, packs, stages, threads, swizzles, and math are unchanged.
resource_notes.api_compliance: coalesced_width is a public keyword argument of tilelang.language.copy. The candidate keeps the baseline imports and uses no extension or private dynamic library, so the source form itself is submission-sandbox compliant.
resource_notes.baseline_codegen: The formal baseline H512/I512 all64 MACA source contains eight statically visible global weight load sites after loop specialization/peeling. Every gate_w, up_w, and down_w load is emitted as *(uint4*), i.e. 16 bytes or eight FP16 elements per thread.
resource_notes.candidate_codegen: The explicit width-16 candidate did not reach MACA source generation or MXCC. During LayoutInference the planner determined an actual vector_size of 8 FP16 elements and rejected coalesced_width=16 because 8 % 16 != 0.
resource_notes.target_limit: For this MACA/xcore1000 path the VectorizePlanner exposes at most the existing 128-bit weight load. The requested 16-FP16/32-byte width is not legal and therefore cannot alter codegen to a 256-bit load.
resource_notes.correctness: Not run because compilation failed before device code generation.
resource_notes.early_stop: Rejected immediately after the required remote compilation evidence. No alternate width and no performance test were attempted.
